index.html.title: TTRPG Wiki to Website Workflow
index.html.description:
So, I've been working on a D&D campaign (as one does), and for it, I've been using Obsidian to work on worldbuilding and have been slowly building my own wiki for the world for the players to use (as one does). Now, my initial idea was for my players to download Obsidian (along with Google Drive or OneDrive for snyc). However, I have recently learned something that is much, much better.And if you're interested in doing the same, let me explain (in the thread below)

This is the hardest and longest step. But it's also the most fun. Download Obsidian from the link above and try it out. It's a text editor that keeps all of your files locally as .md files, which is basically a .txt file with Markdown formatting (the same formatting that Discord uses for italics, bold, and headings). If you want a view of how Obsidian could be use for D&D, check out this video (especially 03:59-16:36 and 22:43-27:51).Vault = Main Obsidian Folder With All Your Files. You don't technically need to do this step, but since the plug-in you're about to install is not made by Obsidian themselves, I would recommend this so that if something does go wrong, you still have all your files somewhere else. (For the record, I've never had anything go wrong in all my testing.) If you have files that you don't want your players to see (possibly because there are spoilers), leave these files out of the duplication.It looks like this:
This is the key to everything.In the plug-in's settings, I would personally recommend turning off "Graph View" and "Backlinks" in Page Features
This is just a preference thing about the end product looks, so feel free to experiment. Also in the 'Export Settings' tab, I would recommend turning on 'Use Relative Header Links' and 'Slugify Paths'.
For the HTML to work, you'll need one file to be your 'homepage.' Figure out which page that will be, and then Add a Property to the file.
Call the Property 'title' and then copy and paste the file's name into the Property
Then, rename the file to 'index'.
Click the 'Export to HTML' button on the sidebar.
Select all the files you want to include in the website, set the Export Mode to 'Local Website', make sure 'Open after export' is on, decide where on in your files the local website will be, and then press 'Export'.
The program should open up your File Explorer to where your local website is (it will be a .HTML file.) If you double click it, you'll get a preview of what your website will look like once it's finished. Feel free to mess with the plug-in's settings to make your website look more like you want it to.Go back into the "Export as HTML" menu in your Obsidian wiki. Make sure the 'Export Mode' is set to 'Online Website' (You will also want to send the website into it's own subfolder in your File Explorer). Check the Settings wheel at the bottom to make sure all the settings are how you want them to be.
This is where things get a little complicated, but bare with me.
Go to GitHub's website and sign up for a free account or log into your own account. Create a New Repository, and name it however you'd like (your eventual website will be [username].github.io/[repository-name]). The visibility will need to be 'Public' (which it is by default).Click 'uploading an existing file.'
Then, drag and drop the HTML files into GitHub. Once its done uploading, scroll down to the bottom of the page and click the green 'Commit changes'.
Go to 'Settings' for your Repository. Click on 'Pages' in the sidebar, then change the 'Source' to 'GitHub Actions'.
Once the page reloads, click 'Static HTML' from the options.
When the page changes, click 'Commit changes...' twice.
Wait a couple minutes. Head back to the 'Pages' tab of 'Settings'. You should see a box that says 'Your site is live at 'https://[username].github.io/[repository-name]/'. If you don't, just give it a few more moments, and then refresh the page.
Click the 'Visit site' to check out your new website!...You're done! Thank you for coming to my TEDTalk. If you have any questions, let me know.

headers.heading: I have discovered a way to easily take an Obsidian Vault and turn it into a website through GitHub Pages.
Step 3: Install the Webpage HTML Export Plug-In to Your Duplicated Wiki
Step 7: Export Your Obsidian (For Real This Time)

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
